attributes.description:
Windows Open Handles — Suspicious Handle Detection (enriched). Detects high-signal open handles held by user-mode processes using the process_open_handles table (requires osquery v5.23.0+).

Scope: this query targets three handle types whose 'name' column is populated by the upstream generator — Key (registry), Mutant (mutex), and File (named pipe). Handle types whose name field is empty by design in the current implementation (Process, Thread, Token — kernel objects without an ObjectNameInformation-resolvable name) are NOT covered here. Detection of cross-process handles into lsass.exe (a credential-theft precursor) would require an upstream feature addition to resolve target identity for Process/Thread handles and is tracked separately.

Detection flags:
- sensitive_registry_flag: Registry Key handles into SAM, SECURITY, or SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa hives held by a non-system process. Credential-extraction targets that legitimate user-mode processes rarely open. IFEO is intentionally excluded — every modern Windows process opens it during startup so the OS can check for an attached debugger, which produces unmanageable false-positive volume.
- suspicious_mutex_flag: Mutant (mutex) handle whose name matches known malware-family or offensive-tooling indicators (Metasploit, Mimikatz, Cobalt Strike, Qbot, Emotet, TrickBot, LockBit, Conti, REvil, RedLine, AgentTesla, LokiBot).
- named_pipe_flag: File handle into a named pipe whose name matches lateral-movement / C2 patterns (PsExec-style service pipes, Cobalt Strike beacon pipes, and RPC pipes for SAM/LSA/Netlogon/Spooler from non-system processes). Chromium 'mojo.*' pipes are intentionally excluded — Edge/WebView2/Chrome use \Device\NamedPipe\...\mojo.<pid>.<tid>.<rand> for legitimate IPC.

Query structure:
- A CTE materializes all (process × handle) tuples without any predicate on process_open_handles columns, so the planner pushes only the required pid constraint into the generator (single equality per pid).
- Flag CASEs and the outer WHERE filter operate on the materialized rowset; hash and authenticode enrichment uses the in-repo CROSS JOIN pattern with TRIM on the path columns.
- System PIDs (0, 4) and processes with empty paths are excluded.

Results are ordered by flag severity (sensitive registry → mutex → named pipe), then by holder pid. On a clean host this query is expected to return zero rows; rows are signal.
attributes.query:
-- Windows Open Handles: Suspicious Handle Detection (enriched)
-- Source: process_open_handles (v5.23.0+).
-- Scope: Key, Mutant, File handle types (those with a populated 'name' column).
-- Performance: detection runs over a materialized CTE; hash + authenticode enrichment
--              is applied only to surviving holder processes via correlated subqueries.

WITH all_handles AS (
    SELECT
        p.pid,
        p.name AS process_name,
        TRIM(p.path) AS process_path,
        p.cmdline,
        p.uid AS user_sid,
        p.parent AS ppid,
        datetime(p.start_time, 'unixepoch') AS process_start_time,
        oh.value AS handle_value,
        oh.type AS handle_type,
        oh.name AS handle_name,
        oh.access AS handle_access,
        oh.attributes AS handle_attributes,
        oh.count AS handle_reference_count,
        oh.raw_pointer_count AS handle_raw_pointer_count,
        oh.error_stage AS handle_error_stage,
        oh.error_code AS handle_error_code
    FROM processes p
    JOIN process_open_handles oh ON oh.pid = p.pid
    WHERE p.pid > 4 AND p.path IS NOT NULL AND p.path != ''
)
SELECT
    ah.pid,
    ah.process_name,
    ah.process_path,
    ah.cmdline,
    ah.user_sid,
    ah.process_start_time,
    ah.ppid,
    (SELECT name FROM processes WHERE pid = ah.ppid) AS parent_name,
    (SELECT TRIM(path) FROM processes WHERE pid = ah.ppid) AS parent_path,
    ah.handle_value,
    ah.handle_type,
    ah.handle_name,
    ah.handle_access,
    ah.handle_attributes,
    ah.handle_reference_count,
    ah.handle_raw_pointer_count,
    ah.handle_error_stage,
    ah.handle_error_code,

    -- Flag 1: Registry Key handles into credential-relevant hives.
    -- IFEO is intentionally excluded — every modern Windows process opens it on
    -- startup so the OS can check for an attached debugger.
    CASE
        WHEN ah.handle_type = 'Key' AND (
            upper(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SAM%'
            OR upper(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SECURITY%'
            OR upper(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SYSTEM\CONTROLSET___\CONTROL\LSA%'
        )
        AND lower(ah.process_name) NOT IN (
            'lsass.exe', 'services.exe', 'wininit.exe', 'system',
            'svchost.exe', 'winlogon.exe', 'smss.exe'
        )
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS sensitive_registry_flag,

    -- Flag 2: known-bad mutex names (Mutant handles)
    CASE
        WHEN ah.handle_type = 'Mutant' AND (
            lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\msf-mutex%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%mimikatz%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%meterpreter%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%powersploit%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%qbot%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%emotet%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%trickbot%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%redline%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%agenttesla%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%lokibot%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%lockbit%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%conti%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%revil%'
        )
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS suspicious_mutex_flag,

    -- Flag 3: named-pipe lateral-movement / C2 patterns.
    -- 'mojo.*' is intentionally excluded — Chromium/Edge/WebView2 use
    -- \Device\NamedPipe\...\mojo.<pid>.<tid>.<rand> for legitimate IPC.
    CASE
        WHEN ah.handle_type = 'File' AND lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '\device\namedpipe\%' AND (
            lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\psexesvc%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\paexec%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\remcom%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\msagent_%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\status_%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\postex_%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\msse-%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\win_svc%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\bypassuac%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\keylogger%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\browser_key_pipe%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\r77%'
            OR (
                (
                    lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\srvsvc'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\samr'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\lsarpc'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\netlogon'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\spoolss'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\wkssvc'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\ntsvcs'
                )
                AND lower(ah.process_name) NOT IN (
                    'services.exe', 'lsass.exe', 'svchost.exe',
                    'spoolsv.exe', 'wininit.exe', 'system'
                )
            )
        )
        THEN 1 ELSE 0
    END AS named_pipe_flag,

    (SELECT md5    FROM hash         WHERE path = ah.process_path) AS process_md5,
    (SELECT sha1   FROM hash         WHERE path = ah.process_path) AS process_sha1,
    (SELECT sha256 FROM hash         WHERE path = ah.process_path) AS process_sha256,
    (SELECT subject_name FROM authenticode WHERE path = ah.process_path) AS signature_signer,
    (SELECT result       FROM authenticode WHERE path = ah.process_path) AS signature_status,
    concat('https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/', (SELECT sha256 FROM hash WHERE path = ah.process_path)) AS vt_link
FROM all_handles ah
WHERE
    (CASE WHEN ah.handle_type = 'Key' AND (
            upper(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SAM%'
            OR upper(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SECURITY%'
            OR upper(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\REGISTRY\MACHINE\SYSTEM\CONTROLSET___\CONTROL\LSA%'
        ) AND lower(ah.process_name) NOT IN (
            'lsass.exe', 'services.exe', 'wininit.exe', 'system',
            'svchost.exe', 'winlogon.exe', 'smss.exe'
        ) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    +
    (CASE WHEN ah.handle_type = 'Mutant' AND (
            lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\msf-mutex%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%mimikatz%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%meterpreter%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%powersploit%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%qbot%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%emotet%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%trickbot%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%redline%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%agenttesla%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%lokibot%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%lockbit%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%conti%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%revil%'
        ) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    +
    (CASE WHEN ah.handle_type = 'File' AND lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '\device\namedpipe\%' AND (
            lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\psexesvc%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\paexec%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\remcom%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\msagent_%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\status_%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\postex_%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\msse-%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\win_svc%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\bypassuac%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\keylogger%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\browser_key_pipe%'
            OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\r77%'
            OR (
                (
                    lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\srvsvc'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\samr'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\lsarpc'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\netlogon'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\spoolss'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\wkssvc'
                    OR lower(ah.handle_name) LIKE '%\ntsvcs'
                )
                AND lower(ah.process_name) NOT IN (
                    'services.exe', 'lsass.exe', 'svchost.exe',
                    'spoolsv.exe', 'wininit.exe', 'system'
                )
            )
        ) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    > 0
ORDER BY
    CASE
        WHEN ah.handle_type = 'Key'    THEN 1
        WHEN ah.handle_type = 'Mutant' THEN 2
        WHEN ah.handle_type = 'File'   THEN 3
        ELSE 4
    END,
    ah.pid;
